subject: Science

subject: Science
description: Develop a simple sketch, drawing, or physical model to illustrate how the shape of an object helps it function as needed to solve a given problem.

subject: Science

subject: Science

description: Count collections of coins (pennies, nickels, and dimes) relating to counting patterns by 1s, 5s, and 10s up to one dollar.
description_plain: Your child can count groups of pennies, nickels, and dimes up to one dollar by counting 1s, 5s, and 10s.

subject: Math
description: Identify and tell the value of a dollar bill, quarter, dime, nickel, and penny.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and say the value of a dollar bill, quarter, dime, nickel, and penny.

subject: Math
description: Tell and write time to the hour and half-hour (including o-clock and half past) using analog and digital clocks.
description_plain: Your child can tell and write time to the hour and half-hour using both clocks with hands and digital clocks.

subject: Math
description: Compare the lengths of three objects using a common measurable attribute.
description_plain: Your child can compare the lengths of three objects by measuring them in the same way.

subject: Math
description: Measure the length of an object as a whole number of same-size, non-standard units from end to end.
description_plain: Your child can measure how long something is by using the same size non-standard units from one end to the other.

subject: Math
description: Compose a geometric shape or solid by combining multiple two-dimensional shapes and/or three-dimensional solids (squares, circles, triangles, rectangles, trapezoids, rhombuses, pentagons, hexagons, octagons, cubes, spheres, cylinders, cones, triangular prisms, and rectangular prisms).
description_plain: Your child can make new shapes or solids by putting together different flat shapes and 3D solids like squares, circles, cubes, and cones.

subject: Math
description: Determine geometric attributes of two-dimensional and three-dimensional shapes (squares, circles, triangles, rectangles, trapezoids, rhombuses, pentagons, hexagons, octagons, cubes, spheres, cylinders, cones, triangular prisms, and rectangular prisms).
description_plain: Your child can find and describe features of flat shapes and solid shapes like squares, triangles, cubes, and spheres.

subject: Math
description: Name and identify solids as three-dimensional (cylinders, cones, triangular prisms, and rectangular prisms).
description_plain: Your child can name and recognize 3D shapes like cylinders, cones, triangular prisms, and rectangular prisms.

subject: Math
description: Name shapes and identify them as two-dimensional (trapezoids, rhombuses, pentagons, hexagons, octagons).
description_plain: Your child can name flat shapes like trapezoids, rhombuses, pentagons, hexagons, and octagons.

subject: Math
description: Identify, create, complete, and extend patterns that are repeating, increasing, and decreasing in a variety of contexts.
description_plain: Your child can find, make, finish, and continue patterns that repeat or change in different ways.

subject: Math
description: Distinguish and use the +, -, and = symbols accurately in an equation.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between +, -, and = signs and use them correctly in math sentences.

subject: Math
description: Solve authentic word problems with subtraction, including unknowns, within 20.
description_plain: Your child can solve real-life subtraction problems with numbers up to 20, including missing numbers.

subject: Math
description: Solve authentic word problems with addition, including three numbers and unknowns, within 20.
description_plain: Your child can solve real-life addition problems with up to three numbers and missing numbers, all within 20.

subject: Math
description: Decompose numbers less than or equal to 20 into pairs in more than one way.
description_plain: Your child can break apart numbers up to 20 into pairs in more than one way.

subject: Math
description: For any number from 1 to 19, find the number that makes 20 when added to the given number, sharing the answer with a model, drawing, or equation.
description_plain: Your child can find the number that adds to any number from 1 to 19 to make 20, and show the answer with a drawing or equation.

subject: Math
description: Automatically add and subtract within 10.
description_plain: Your child can quickly add and subtract numbers within 10.

subject: Math
description: Partition circles and rectangles into two and four equal shares using the language halves and fourths.
description_plain: Your child can divide circles and rectangles into two or four equal parts and use words like halves and fourths.

subject: Math
description: Mentally add or subtract 10 to or from a given two-digit number and explain the reasoning used.
description_plain: Your child can add or subtract 10 in their head to a two-digit number and explain how they did it.

subject: Math
description: Subtract multiples of 10 within 100 using concrete models, drawings, and strategies that reflect an understanding of place value.
description_plain: Your child can subtract tens within 100 using objects, drawings, or strategies that show they understand place value.

subject: Math
description: Add within 100 using a two-digit number and a one-digit number. Use concrete models, drawings, and strategies that reflect an understanding of place value.
description_plain: Your child can add a two-digit number and a one-digit number within 100 using objects, drawings, or strategies that show place value.

subject: Math
description: Compare two two-digit numbers using symbols >, <, and =. Justify comparisons based on the value of tens and ones.
description_plain: Your child can compare two two-digit numbers using >, <, and = signs and explain their thinking based on tens and ones.

subject: Math
description: Demonstrate that the two digits of a two-digit number represent a composition of some tens and some ones.
description_plain: Your child understands that a two-digit number is made of some tens and some ones.

subject: Math
description: Skip count forward and backward by 5s and 10s from multiples and recognize the patterns of up to 10 skip counts.
description_plain: Your child can skip count forward and backward by 5s and 10s from multiples and notice the patterns up to 10 times.

subject: Math
description: Recognize and verbally label arrangements, without counting, for briefly shown collections up to 20 (e.g., "I saw 16." How do you know?" "I saw 10 and 6, that is 16.").
description_plain: Your child can quickly say how many are in a group up to 20 without counting one by one.

subject: Math
description: Represent several objects with a written numeral up to 120.
description_plain: Your child can write numbers to show how many objects there are, up to 120.

subject: Math
description: Count backward by ones and tens from a given number within 120.
description_plain: Your child can count backward by ones and tens from any number up to 120.

subject: Math
description: Count forward by ones and tens from any given point within 120.
description_plain: Your child can count forward by ones and tens starting from any number up to 120.

subject: Math
description: Learners can use prior knowledge and experiences to explain their thinking.
description_plain: Your child can use what they already know to explain their thinking.

subject: Math
description: Learners can use prior knowledge and experiences to explain their thinking.
description_plain: Your child can use what they already know to explain their thinking.

subject: Math
description: Learners can identify and use strategies to problem-solve situations and determine an appropriate solution.
description_plain: Your child can find ways to solve problems and pick the best answer.

subject: Math
description: Analyze data and interpret the results to solve one-step comparison problems using information from the graphs.
description_plain: Your child can look at data from graphs and use it to solve simple comparison problems.

subject: Math
description: Generate data and create line plots marked in whole-number units.
description_plain: Your child can collect data and make line plots using whole number marks.

subject: Math
description: Formulate questions and collect, organize, and represent data with up to four categories using single unit scaled picture and bar graphs.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions, gather information, sort it into up to four groups, and show it with picture or bar graphs.

subject: Math
description: Count collections of money (quarters, dimes, nickels, and pennies) relating to counting patterns by 1s, 5s, and 10s up to one dollar.
description_plain: Your child can count money with quarters, dimes, nickels, and pennies up to one dollar by counting 1s, 5s, and 10s.

subject: Math
description: Tell and write time to the nearest five minutes (including quarter after and quarter to) with a.m. and p.m. using analog and digital clocks.
description_plain: Your child can tell and write time to the nearest five minutes, including quarter after and quarter to, using a.m. and p.m. on both clocks with hands and digital clocks.

subject: Math
description: Estimate and measure to determine how much longer one object is than another, expressing the difference with a standard unit of measurement.
description_plain: Your child can guess and measure how much longer one object is than another and say the difference using standard units.

subject: Math
description: Measure the length of an object using two different standard units of measurement. Describe how the two measurements relate to the size of the units chosen.
description_plain: Your child can measure how long something is using two different standard units and explain how the measurements relate to the size of the units.

subject: Math
description: Compose geometric shapes having specified geometric attributes, such as a given number of edges, angles, faces, vertices, and/or sides.
description_plain: Your child can put together shapes that have a certain number of edges, angles, faces, or corners.

subject: Math
description: Identify two-dimensional shapes found within three-dimensional shapes.
description_plain: Your child can find flat shapes inside solid shapes.

subject: Math
description: Identify two-dimensional shapes (parallelograms and quadrilaterals).
description_plain: Your child can recognize flat shapes like parallelograms and quadrilaterals.

subject: Math
description: Identify a group of objects from 0 to 20 as even or odd by showing even numbers as a sum of two equal parts.
description_plain: Your child can tell if a group of 0 to 20 objects is even or odd by showing even numbers as two equal parts added together.

subject: Math
description: Use repeated addition to find the total number of objects arranged in a rectangular array.
description_plain: Your child can add the same number repeatedly to find the total in a rectangle-shaped group of objects.

subject: Math
description: Solve one- and two-step authentic word problems with subtraction within 100, including the use of unknowns.
description_plain: Your child can solve real-life subtraction problems with one or two steps, including problems with missing numbers, up to 100.

subject: Math
description: Solve one- and two-step authentic word problems with addition within 100, including the use of unknowns.
description_plain: Your child can solve real-life addition problems with one or two steps, including problems with missing numbers, up to 100.

subject: Math
description: Apply the properties of operations to solve addition and subtraction equations within 100 and justify thinking.
description_plain: Your child can use rules about adding and subtracting to solve problems up to 100 and explain their thinking.

subject: Math
description: Automatically add and subtract within 20.
description_plain: Your child can quickly add and subtract numbers up to 20.

subject: Math
description: Recognize that partitioning shapes into more equal shares creates smaller shares.
description_plain: Your child understands that cutting shapes into more equal parts makes each part smaller.

subject: Math
description: Recognize that identical wholes can be equally divided in different ways.
description_plain: Your child understands that the same whole shape can be divided equally in different ways.

subject: Math
description: Partition circles and rectangles into two, three, or four equal shares. Describe the shares using the language of halves, thirds, fourths, half of, a third of, and a fourth of.
description_plain: Your child can divide circles and rectangles into 2, 3, or 4 equal parts and use words like halves, thirds, and fourths to describe them.

subject: Math
description: Mentally add or subtract 10 or 100 to or from a given number between 100 and 900.
description_plain: Your child can add or subtract 10 or 100 in their head to numbers between 100 and 900.

subject: Math
description: Subtract within 100 using place value strategies and/or the relationship between addition and subtraction.
description_plain: Your child can subtract numbers up to 100 using place value ideas or by thinking about addition.

subject: Math
description: Add within 100 using place value strategies and/or the relationship between addition and subtraction.
description_plain: Your child can add numbers up to 100 using place value ideas or by thinking about subtraction.

subject: Math
description: Compare two three-digit numbers using symbols >, <, and =. Justify comparisons based on the value of hundreds, tens, and ones.
description_plain: Your child can compare two three-digit numbers using >, <, or = and explain by looking at hundreds, tens, and ones.

subject: Math
description: Understand that the three digits of a three-digit number represent a composition of some hundreds, some tens, and some ones.
description_plain: Your child understands that a three-digit number is made of hundreds, tens, and ones.

subject: Math
description: Skip count forward and backward by 2s and 100s and recognize the patterns of skip counts.
description_plain: Your child can count forward and backward by 2s and 100s and notice the patterns.

subject: Math
description: Read and write numbers up to 1000 using standard, word, and expanded forms.
description_plain: Your child can read and write numbers up to 1000 in normal form, word form, and expanded form.

subject: Math
description: Count backward from any given number within 1000.
description_plain: Your child can count backward from any number up to 1000.

subject: Math
description: Count forward from any given number within 1000.
description_plain: Your child can count forward from any number up to 1000.

subject: Math
description: Learners can use prior knowledge and experiences to explain their thinking.
description_plain: Your child can explain their thinking by using what they already know and have experienced.

subject: Math
description: Learners can use prior knowledge and experiences to explain their thinking.
description_plain: Your child can explain their thinking by using what they already know and have experienced.

subject: Math
description: Learners can identify and use strategies to problem-solve situations and determine an appropriate solution.
description_plain: Your child can find and use ways to solve problems and choose the best answer.

subject: Math
description: Analyze data and make simple statements to solve one- and two-step problems using information from the graphs.
description_plain: Your child can look at data on graphs and use it to solve one- and two-step problems.

subject: Math
description: Generate data and create line plots marked in whole numbers, halves, and fourths of a unit.
description_plain: Your child can collect data and make line plots that show whole numbers, halves, and fourths.

subject: Math
description: Formulate questions to collect, organize, and represent data with more than four categories using scaled picture and bar graphs.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions to gather and organize data with more than four groups and show it using picture or bar graphs.

subject: Math
description: Find the area of a rectangle with whole-number side lengths by modeling with unit squares; show that area can be additive and is the same as would be found by multiplying the side lengths.
description_plain: Your child can find the area of a rectangle by counting squares and understand that area is the same as multiplying the sides.

subject: Math
description: Recognize area as an attribute of plane figures and understand concepts of area measurement.
description_plain: Your child understands that area is how much space a flat shape covers and knows how to measure it.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems involving the perimeters of rectangles given the side lengths or when given the perimeter and unknown side length(s).
description_plain: Your child can solve problems about the perimeter of rectangles when they know the sides or the total perimeter and some sides.

subject: Math
description: Solve authentic word problems involving dollar bills, quarters, dimes, nickels, and pennies using the $ and ¢ symbols appropriately.
description_plain: Your child can solve real money problems using dollar bills and coins and use $ and ¢ correctly.

subject: Math
description: Solve elapsed time authentic word problems on the hour and the half-hour, using a variety of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can solve real problems about elapsed time on the hour and half-hour using different methods.

subject: Math
description: Tell and write time to the nearest minute and measure time intervals in minutes.
description_plain: Your child can tell and write time to the nearest minute and measure how long things take in minutes.

subject: Math
description: Measure and estimate liquid volumes and masses of objects using standard units. Solve one-step authentic word problems involving masses on volume given in the same units.
description_plain: Your child can measure and estimate liquid amounts and object weights using standard units and solve simple problems about them.

subject: Math
description: Measure lengths using rulers marked with halves and fourths of an inch.
description_plain: Your child can measure lengths using rulers that show halves and fourths of an inch.

subject: Math
description: Identify lines of symmetry in quadrilaterals.
description_plain: Your child can find lines of symmetry in four-sided shapes.

subject: Math
description: Sort quadrilaterals into categories based on attributes.
description_plain: Your child can group four-sided shapes based on their features.

subject: Math
description: In two-dimensional shapes, identify lines, angles (right, acute, obtuse), and perpendicular and parallel lines.
description_plain: Your child can find lines, different types of angles, and parallel or perpendicular lines in flat shapes.

subject: Math
description: Identify arithmetic patterns and explain them using the properties of operations.
description_plain: Your child can find number patterns and explain them using rules about adding and multiplying.

subject: Math
description: Use strategies and visual models to solve authentic word problems with division within 100, including unknowns, using grouping models and equations.
description_plain: Your child can use pictures and strategies to solve real division problems with numbers up to 100, including problems with missing numbers.

subject: Math
description: Use strategies and visual models to solve authentic word problems with multiplication within 100, including unknowns, using grouping models and equations.
description_plain: Your child can use pictures and grouping to solve real multiplication word problems with numbers up to 100, including finding missing numbers.

subject: Math
description: Solve two-step authentic word problems using addition and subtraction within 1000, including equations with a letter as an unknown.
description_plain: Your child can solve two-step word problems using addition and subtraction with numbers up to 1000, including using letters to stand for unknown numbers.

subject: Math
description: Apply the properties of operations to solve multiplication and division equations and justify thinking.
description_plain: Your child can use rules about multiplication and division to solve problems and explain their thinking.

subject: Math
description: Using mental strategies, multiply and divide basic facts within 100. Automatically multiply and divide up to 5 x 5 and 10s facts.
description_plain: Your child can quickly multiply and divide basic facts within 100 in their head, especially facts up to 5 x 5 and multiples of 10.

subject: Math
description: Compare fractions of the same whole having the same numerators or denominators, using symbols >, <, and = by reasoning about their size (fractions should be limited to denominators of 2, 3, 4, 6, and 8 and should not exceed the whole).
description_plain: Your child can compare fractions with the same top number or bottom number using >, <, and = by thinking about their size, with denominators like 2, 3, 4, 6, and 8.

subject: Math
description: Recognize whole numbers as fractions and express fractions that are equivalent to whole numbers.
description_plain: Your child understands that whole numbers can be shown as fractions and can write fractions that equal whole numbers.

subject: Math
description: Represent equivalent fractions using visual representations and number lines.
description_plain: Your child can show fractions that are equal using pictures and number lines.

subject: Math
description: Represent and understand a fraction as a number on a number line.
description_plain: Your child can show and understand a fraction as a point on a number line.

subject: Math
description: Partition two-dimensional figures into equal areas and express the area of each part as a unit fraction of the whole. Describe using the language of sixths, eighths, a sixth of, and an eighth of.
description_plain: Your child can divide flat shapes into equal parts and say the size of each part using fractions like sixths and eighths.

subject: Math
description: Multiply one-digit whole numbers by multiples of 10 within 100.
description_plain: Your child can multiply one-digit numbers by 10, 20, 30, and so on, up to 100.

subject: Math
description: Add and subtract within 1000 using place value strategies, algorithms, and/or the relationship between addition and subtraction.
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract numbers up to 1000 using place value ideas, steps, or the connection between adding and subtracting.

subject: Math
description: Apply place value understanding to round whole numbers to the nearest 10 or 100.
description_plain: Your child can round whole numbers to the nearest 10 or 100 by understanding place value.

subject: Math
description: Compare two four-digit numbers using symbols >, <, and =. Justify comparisons based on the value of thousands, hundreds, tens, and ones.
description_plain: Your child can compare two numbers up to 4 digits using >, <, and = and explain their answer by looking at thousands, hundreds, tens, and ones.

subject: Math
description: Read and write numbers up to 10,000 using objects or visual representations, including standard, word, and expanded forms.
description_plain: Your child can read and write numbers up to 10,000 using objects, pictures, words, and expanded form.

subject: Math
description: Learners can reason logically based on experience and knowledge, citing evidence to support their reasoning and conclusions.
description_plain: Your child can think carefully and use what they know to explain their answers and ideas.

subject: Math
description: Learners can make connections and summarize related ideas using supporting evidence.
description_plain: Your child can connect ideas and explain them clearly using examples and reasons.

subject: Math
description: Learners can develop and carry out a logical plan to problem-solve situations, reflect on the reasonableness of solutions, and explore alternate strategies with guidance.
description_plain: Your child can make a plan to solve problems, check if answers make sense, and try different ways to solve with some help.

subject: Math
description: Utilize graphs and diagrams to represent and solve authentic word problems using the four operations involving whole numbers, benchmark fractions, and decimals.
description_plain: Your child can use graphs and pictures to solve real word problems with addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division using whole numbers, simple fractions, and decimals.

subject: Math
description: Generate data and create line plots to display a data set of unit fractions (½, ¼, ⅛). Solve problems involving addition and subtraction of fractions by using information presented in line plots.
description_plain: Your child can collect data and make line plots with fractions like 1/2, 1/4, and 1/8, then solve addition and subtraction problems using the plots.

subject: Math
description: Formulate questions to collect, organize, and represent data to reason with math and across disciplines.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions, gather data, organize it, and use it to solve math problems and learn about other subjects.

subject: Math
description: Recognize angle measures as additive and solve addition and subtraction problems to find unknown angles on a diagram.
description_plain: Your child can understand that angles add up and solve problems by adding or subtracting angles to find missing ones in pictures.

subject: Math
description: Measure angles in whole-number degrees using a protractor. Using a protractor and ruler, draw angles of a specified measure.
description_plain: Your child can measure angles with a protractor and draw angles of a certain size using a protractor and ruler.

subject: Math
description: Apply the area and perimeter formulas for rectangles, including connected rectangular figures, in problems.
description_plain: Your child can use formulas to find the area and perimeter of rectangles, including shapes made of connected rectangles.

subject: Math
description: Solve authentic word problems involving dollar bills, quarters, dimes, nickels, and pennies using $ and ¢ symbols and decimal notation appropriately.
description_plain: Your child can solve money problems using dollars and coins, writing amounts with $ and ¢ symbols and decimals correctly.

subject: Math
description: Identify and use the appropriate tools, operations, and units of measurement, both customary and metric, to solve problems involving time, length, weight, mass, and capacity.
description_plain: Your child can choose the right tools, math operations, and units to solve problems about time, length, weight, mass, and capacity using both customary and metric systems.

subject: Math
description: Generate simple conversions from a larger unit to a smaller unit to solve authentic problems within a single system of measurement, both customary and metric systems.
description_plain: Your child can change larger units to smaller units within the same measurement system to solve real problems, using both customary and metric units.

subject: Math
description: Know the relative sizes of measurement units within one system of units including km, m, cm; kg, g; lb., oz.; l, ml; hr., min., sec. Record measurement equivalents in a two-column table.
description_plain: Your child knows how big different measurement units are in one system, like kilometers, meters, and centimeters, and can write their equivalents in a table.

subject: Math
description: Draw lines of symmetry in two-dimensional figures.
description_plain: Your child can draw lines of symmetry on flat shapes.

subject: Math
description: Classify two-dimensional figures based on the presence or absence of parallel or perpendicular lines, or the presence or absence of angles of specified size.
description_plain: Your child can sort flat shapes by whether they have parallel or perpendicular lines or certain angle sizes.

subject: Math
description: Identify, label, and draw points, lines, line segments, rays, and angles (right, acute, obtuse).
description_plain: Your child can find, name, and draw points, lines, line segments, rays, and different types of angles like right, acute, and obtuse.

subject: Math
description: Generate a number or shape pattern that follows a given rule while identifying apparent features of the pattern that were not explicit in the rule itself. (e.g., Given a rule "add 3" and the starting number of 1, generate terms in the resulting sequence and observe that the terms appear to alternate between odd and even numbers.)
description_plain: Your child can create number or shape patterns following a rule and notice extra features in the pattern that the rule doesn’t say.

subject: Math
description: Interpret multiplication equations as a comparison. Represent multiplicative comparisons as multiplication equations.
description_plain: Your child can understand multiplication sentences as comparisons and write multiplication equations to show these comparisons.

subject: Math
description: Find factor pairs and multiples within the range of 1-36 while classifying numbers as prime or composite.
description_plain: Your child can find pairs of numbers that multiply to numbers between 1 and 36 and decide if numbers are prime or composite.

subject: Math
description: Solve multi-step authentic word problems using the four operations, including problems with interpreted remainders. Represent problems using equations, including a symbol as an unknown.
description_plain: Your child can solve multi-step real-world problems using all four operations, including problems with remainders, and write equations with unknowns to show the problems.

subject: Math
description: Identify and apply the properties of operations for addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division and justify thinking.
description_plain: Your child can use rules about addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division to solve problems and explain their thinking.

subject: Math
description: Automatically multiply and divide through 10 x 10.
description_plain: Your child can quickly multiply and divide numbers up to 10 x 10.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems by multiplying fractions and whole numbers using visual fraction models (proper and improper fractions limited to denominators of 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10, 12, and 100).
description_plain: Your child can solve problems by multiplying fractions and whole numbers using pictures, with fractions having denominators like 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10, 12, and 100.

subject: Math
description: Solve authentic word problems by adding and subtracting fractions and mixed numbers with like denominators (proper and improper fractions limited to denominators of 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10, 12, and 100).
description_plain: Your child can solve real problems by adding and subtracting fractions and mixed numbers with the same bottom number, using fractions with denominators like 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10, 12, and 100.

subject: Math
description: Compare and order fractions having, unlike numerators or denominators. Record comparisons using the symbols >, <, and =. Justify using a visual fraction model (proper and improper fractions limited to denominators of 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10, 12, and 100).
description_plain: Your child can compare and order fractions with different top or bottom numbers using >, <, and =, and explain their answers using pictures, with denominators like 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10, 12, and 100.

subject: Math
description: Demonstrate how equivalent fractions are generated by multiplying a fraction equivalent to 1 or the properties of multiplication (proper and improper fractions limited to denominators of 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10, 12, and 100).
description_plain: Your child can show how equivalent fractions are made by multiplying by fractions equal to 1 or using multiplication rules, with denominators like 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10, 12, and 100.

subject: Math
description: Generate equivalent fractions using numerical representations, visual representations, and number lines (proper and improper fractions limited to denominators of 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10, 12, and 100).
description_plain: Your child can find fractions that are equal to each other using numbers, pictures, and number lines with denominators like 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10, 12, and 100.

subject: Math
description: Explain and demonstrate how a mixed number is equivalent to a fraction greater than one and how a fraction greater than one is equal to a mixed number using visual fraction models and reasoning strategies (proper and improper fractions limited to denominators of 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10, 12, and 100).
description_plain: Your child understands that a mixed number is the same as a fraction bigger than one, and can show this using pictures and reasoning with certain denominators.

subject: Math
description: Express equivalent fractions with a denominator of 10 and a denominator of 100 to generate a decimal notation.
description_plain: Your child can write fractions with denominators of 10 and 100 as decimals.

subject: Math
description: Find whole-number quotients and remainders with up to four-digit dividends and one-digit divisors using place value strategies. Show and justify the calculation using equations, rectangular arrays, and models.
description_plain: Your child can divide numbers up to four digits by one-digit numbers, find the remainder, and explain their work using equations and models.

subject: Math
description: Multiply a whole number of up to four digits by a one-digit whole number and multiply two two-digit numbers. Show and justify the calculation using equations, rectangular arrays, and models.
description_plain: Your child can multiply a number up to four digits by a one-digit number and multiply two two-digit numbers, showing and explaining their work with equations and models.

subject: Math
description: Add and subtract multi-digit whole numbers to the one millions place using strategies, including the algorithm.
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract large whole numbers up to one million using different methods, including the standard algorithm.

subject: Math
description: Apply place value understanding to round multi-digit whole numbers to any place.
description_plain: Your child can round large whole numbers to any place value.

subject: Math
description: Compare two numbers to the millions place and decimals to the hundredths place, using symbols >, <, and =. Justify comparisons based on the value of the digits.
description_plain: Your child can compare two numbers up to one million and decimals to the hundredths place using >, <, and =, and explain why.

subject: Math
description: Understand that in a multi-digit whole number, a digit in one place represents ten times what it represents in the place to its right.
description_plain: Your child understands that in a multi-digit number, each digit is worth ten times more than the digit to its right.

subject: Math
description: Read numbers to the millions place, including word, standard, and expanded form. Write numbers to the millions place, including standard and expanded form.
description_plain: Your child can read and write numbers up to one million in words, standard form, and expanded form.

subject: Math
description: Learners can reason logically based on experience and knowledge, citing evidence to support their reasoning and conclusions.
description_plain: Your child can think carefully and explain their answers using what they know and evidence.

subject: Math
description: Learners can make connections and summarize related ideas using supporting evidence.
description_plain: Your child can connect ideas and explain them clearly using evidence.

subject: Math
description: Learners can develop and carry out a logical plan to problem-solve situations, reflect on the reasonableness of solutions, and explore alternate strategies with guidance.
description_plain: Your child can make a plan to solve problems, check if their answers make sense, and try different ways to solve problems with some help.

subject: Math
description: Utilize graphs and diagrams to represent, analyze, and solve authentic word problems using information presented in one or more tables or line plots including whole numbers, fractions, and decimals.
description_plain: Your child can use graphs and diagrams to understand and solve real-life word problems with whole numbers, fractions, and decimals.

subject: Math
description: Generate data and create line plots to display a data set of unit fractions (½, ¼, ⅛). Use grade-level operations for fractions to solve problems involving information presented in line plots.
description_plain: Your child can collect data and make line plots with fractions like 1/2, 1/4, and 1/8, then use math with fractions to solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Recognize volume as an attribute of rectangular prisms and measure volume by counting unit cubes.
description_plain: Your child knows that volume is how much space a box-shaped object takes up and can measure it by counting cubes.

subject: Math
description: Find the area and perimeter of a rectangle, including connected rectangular figures, with fractional side lengths.
description_plain: Your child can find the area and perimeter of rectangles, including shapes made of rectangles, even when sides have fractions.

subject: Math
description: Generate conversions among different-sized standard measurement units within a given measurement system, both customary and metric systems. Use these conversions in solving multi-step, authentic word problems.
description_plain: Your child can change measurements between different units in the same system and use these to solve multi-step real-world problems.

subject: Math
description: Form ordered pairs and graph points in the first quadrant on the coordinate plane to solve authentic word problems.
description_plain: Your child can make pairs of numbers and plot points on a graph to solve real-world problems.

subject: Math
description: Identify the x-coordinate and y-coordinate to graph and name points in the first quadrant of the coordinate plane.
description_plain: Your child can find and use the x and y coordinates to plot and name points on a graph in the first quadrant.

subject: Math
description: Classify two-dimensional figures in a hierarchy based on properties.
description_plain: Your child can group flat shapes by their properties in a way that shows how they are related.

subject: Math
description: Generate two numerical patterns using two given rules and form ordered pairs consisting of corresponding terms from the two patterns. (Graphing on a coordinate plane).
description_plain: Your child can create two number patterns from rules and pair their matching terms to make points for graphing.

subject: Math
description: Find factor pairs and multiples within the range of 1-100 while classifying numbers as prime or composite.
description_plain: Your child can find pairs of factors and multiples between 1 and 100 and decide if numbers are prime or composite.

subject: Math
description: Write simple expressions that record calculations with numbers. Interpret numerical expressions without evaluating them.
description_plain: Your child can write simple math expressions to show calculations and understand what they mean without solving them.

subject: Math
description: Analyze problems using the order of operations to solve and evaluate expressions while justifying thinking.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems using the correct order of operations and explain their thinking.

subject: Math
description: Automatically multiply and divide through 12 x 12.
description_plain: Your child can quickly multiply and divide numbers up to 12 by 12.

subject: Math
description: Solve authentic word problems by multiplying fractions and mixed numbers using visual fraction models and equations.
description_plain: Your child can solve real-life problems by multiplying fractions and mixed numbers using pictures and equations.

subject: Math
description: Solve authentic word problems by adding and subtracting fractions and mixed numbers with unlike denominators using visual fraction models and equations.
description_plain: Your child can solve real-life problems by adding and subtracting fractions and mixed numbers with different denominators using pictures and equations.

subject: Math
description: Explain why multiplying a given number by a fraction greater than one results in a product greater than the given number and explain why multiplying a given number by a fraction less than one results in a product smaller than the given number.
description_plain: Your child can explain why multiplying by a fraction bigger than one makes a number bigger, and multiplying by a fraction less than one makes it smaller.

subject: Math
description: Generate equivalent forms of commonly used fractions and decimals (e.g., halves, fourths, fifths, tenths).
description_plain: Your child can find different forms of common fractions and decimals, like halves, fourths, fifths, and tenths.

subject: Math
description: Explain patterns in the number of zeros of the product when multiplying a number by powers of 10. Explain patterns in the placement of the decimal point when a decimal is multiplied or divided by a power of 10. Use whole-number exponents to denote powers of 10.
description_plain: Your child can explain patterns in zeros when multiplying by 10, 100, etc., and how the decimal point moves when multiplying or dividing by these powers of 10.

subject: Math
description: Find whole-number quotients and remainders with up to four-digit dividends and two-digit divisors using place value strategies. Show and justify the calculation by using equations, rectangular arrays, and/or area models.
description_plain: Your child can divide numbers up to four digits by two-digit numbers, find the remainder, and explain their work using equations and models.

subject: Math
description: Use concrete models, drawings, place value strategies, properties of operations and/or relationships to add, subtract, and multiply decimals to hundredths.
description_plain: Your child can add, subtract, and multiply decimals to the hundredths place using models, drawings, and place value strategies.

subject: Math
description: Multiply multi-digit whole numbers using strategies flexibly, including the algorithm.
description_plain: Your child can multiply large whole numbers using different methods, including the standard algorithm.

subject: Math
description: Apply place value understanding to round decimals to any place.
description_plain: Your child can round decimals to any place value.

subject: Math
description: Compare two decimals to the thousandths place using symbols >, <, and =. Justify comparisons based on the value of the digits.
description_plain: Your child can compare two decimals to the thousandths place using >, <, and =, and explain why.

subject: Math
description: Understand that in a multi-digit whole number, a digit in one place represents ten times as much as it represents in the place to its right and 1/10 of what it represents in the place to its left.
description_plain: Your child understands that in a multi-digit number, each digit is worth ten times the digit to its right and one-tenth the digit to its left.

subject: Math
description: Read and write decimals to the thousandths including standard, word, and expanded forms.
description_plain: Your child can read and write decimals to the thousandths place in words, standard form, and expanded form.

subject: Math
description: Learners can reason logically based on experience and knowledge, citing evidence to support their reasoning and conclusions.
description_plain: Your child can think carefully and explain their answers using what they know and evidence.

subject: Math
description: Learners can make connections and summarize related ideas using supporting evidence.
description_plain: Your child can connect ideas and explain them clearly using evidence.

subject: Math
description: Learners can develop and carry out a logical plan to problem-solve situations, reflect on the reasonableness of solutions, and explore alternate strategies with guidance.
description_plain: Your child can make a clear plan to solve problems, check if the answers make sense, and try different ways to solve them with some help.

subject: Math
description: Display numerical data in plots on a number line, including dot plots and histograms. Describe any overall patterns in data, such as gaps, clusters, and skews.
description_plain: Your child can show number data on a number line using dot plots and histograms, and talk about patterns like gaps or groups in the data.

subject: Math
description: Identify outliers by observation and describe their effect on measures of center and variability. Justify which measures would be appropriate to answer a statistical question.
description_plain: Your child can spot unusual data points and explain how they affect averages and spread, and decide which measurements best answer a question.

subject: Math
description: Calculate measures of center (median and mean) and variability (range and mean absolute deviation) to answer a statistical question. Identify mode(s) if they exist.
description_plain: Your child can find the middle, average, range, and how much data varies to answer questions, and find the most common number if there is one.

subject: Math
description: Write a statistical question that can be answered using measures of center or variability of a data set.
description_plain: Your child can write a question that can be answered by looking at averages or how data varies.

subject: Math
description: Represent three-dimensional figures using nets made up of rectangles and triangles (right prisms and pyramids whose bases are triangles and rectangles). Calculate the surface area of prisms with rectangular and triangular bases using nets, including authentic problems.
description_plain: Your child can draw flat shapes that fold into 3D figures like prisms and pyramids, and find the surface area by using these drawings to solve real problems.

subject: Math
description: Draw polygons in the coordinate plane given coordinates for the vertices. Determine the length of a side joining points with the same first or second coordinate, including authentic problems.
description_plain: Your child can draw shapes on a grid using points and find the length of sides when points share an x or y coordinate, including real-life problems.

subject: Math
description: Identify and position ordered pairs of rational numbers in all four quadrants of a coordinate plane.
description_plain: Your child can find and place points with fractions or decimals on all parts of a coordinate grid.

subject: Math
description: Describe the concept of volume of a right rectangular prism. Apply given formulas to calculate the volume of right rectangular prisms, including fractional edge lengths, including authentic problems.
description_plain: Your child understands volume as the space inside a box shape and can use formulas to find volume, even when sides are fractions, solving real problems.

subject: Math
description: Derive the relationship of the areas of triangles using the area of rectangles. Calculate the areas of triangles and quadrilaterals by composing and/or decomposing them into rectangles and triangles, including authentic problems.
description_plain: Your child can find the area of triangles and four-sided shapes by breaking them into rectangles and triangles, and solve real problems using this.

subject: Math
description: Write a statement of inequality of the form x > c or the form x < c to represent a constraint or condition. Recognize that inequalities of the form x > c or the form x < c have infinitely many solutions; represent solutions of such inequalities on number line diagrams.
description_plain: Your child can write inequalities like x > c or x < c to show limits, understand these have many solutions, and show these solutions on a number line.

subject: Math
description: Write and solve equations of the form x + p = q and px = q for cases in which p and q are non-negative whole numbers or decimals, including authentic problems.
description_plain: Your child can write and solve simple equations like x + p = q or p x = q when p and q are whole numbers or decimals, including real-life problems.

subject: Math
description: Describe the concept of a solution of an equation and an inequality. Determine whether a given number is a solution to an equation or an inequality.
description_plain: Your child understands what it means to solve an equation or inequality and can tell if a number solves it.

subject: Math
description: Identify when two expressions are equivalent. Apply the properties of operations to generate equivalent expressions.
description_plain: Your child can tell when two math expressions mean the same thing and use math rules to create equal expressions.

subject: Math
description: Read and evaluate algebraic expressions, including expressions with whole number exponents and grouping symbols. Write algebraic expressions to represent simple and authentic situations.
description_plain: Your child can read and solve math expressions with exponents and grouping symbols, and write expressions to match simple real situations.

subject: Math
description: Read, write, and evaluate numerical expressions including expressions with whole number exponents and grouping symbols.
description_plain: Your child can read, write, and solve number expressions that include exponents and grouping symbols.

subject: Math
description: Convert measurement units within and between measurement systems using ratio reasoning given conversion factors.
description_plain: Your child can change measurements within and between systems using ratios and given conversion numbers.

subject: Math
description: Calculate a percent of a quantity as a rate per 100. Solve problems using ratio reasoning involving finding the whole when given a part and the percent.
description_plain: Your child can find a percent of a number and solve problems where you find the whole amount when given a part and a percent.

subject: Math
description: Make and use tables of equivalent ratios, tape diagrams, double number line diagrams, and equations to solve problems involving ratios, rates, and unit rates, including authentic problems.
description_plain: Your child can use tables, pictures, and equations to solve problems about ratios, rates, and unit rates, including real-life examples.

subject: Math
description: Describe and calculate a unit rate when given a ratio relationship between two quantities using rate language and visual models.
description_plain: Your child can explain and find a unit rate from a ratio using words and pictures.

subject: Math
description: Describe the concept of a ratio relationship between two quantities using ratio language and visual models.
description_plain: Your child understands what a ratio is between two amounts and can explain it using words and pictures.

subject: Math
description: Determine the greatest common factor of two whole numbers less than or equal to 100 and the least common multiple of two whole numbers less than or equal to 12.
description_plain: Your child can find the greatest common factor of two numbers up to 100 and the least common multiple of two numbers up to 12.

subject: Math
description: Apply multiplication and division of fractions and decimals to solve and interpret problems using visual models, including authentic problems.
description_plain: Your child can multiply and divide fractions and decimals using pictures and solve real problems.

subject: Math
description: Add and subtract fractions and decimals up to the hundredths place, including authentic problems.
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract fractions and decimals up to two decimal places and solve real problems.

subject: Math
description: Divide multi-digit whole numbers up to four-digit dividends and two-digit divisors using strategies or procedures.
description_plain: Your child can divide large whole numbers using different methods.

subject: Math
description: Write, interpret, and explain statements of order for rational numbers on a number line and in authentic contexts.
description_plain: Your child can write and explain how numbers compare on a number line and in real situations.

subject: Math
description: Explain and show the relationship between non-zero rational numbers and their opposites using horizontal and vertical number lines, including authentic problems. Use rational numbers to represent quantities in authentic contexts and explain the meaning of 0 in certain situations.
description_plain: Your child can explain how numbers and their opposites relate using number lines and use numbers to show real quantities, including what zero means.

subject: Math
description: Learners can reason logically, citing evidence to evaluate and explain what they see, think, and conclude through exploration and justification.
description_plain: Your child can think carefully, use evidence to explain what they notice, think, and decide during learning.

subject: Math
description: Learners can create connections within and across concepts and provide examples of how they relate to other learning and ideas using supporting evidence.
description_plain: Your child can connect ideas within math and to other subjects, giving examples and reasons.

subject: Math
description: Learners can analyze information and formulate a flexible, systematic plan to problem-solve authentic situations and reflect on the reasonableness of the solution, making revisions when necessary.
description_plain: Your child can look at information, make a clear plan to solve real problems, check if answers make sense, and change the plan if needed.

subject: Math
description: Develop a probability model to find theoretical probabilities of independent compound events.
description_plain: Your child can create a model to find the chance of two independent events happening together.

subject: Math
description: Develop a probability model to find probabilities of theoretical events and contrast probabilities from an experimental model.
description_plain: Your child can build a model to find the chance of events happening and compare it to actual results.

subject: Math
description: Analyze and draw inferences about a population using single and multiple random samples by using given measures of center and variability for the numerical data set.
description_plain: Your child can study groups of data from one or more random samples and use averages and spread to understand the whole group.

subject: Math
description: Identify the strengths and weaknesses of a population sample including bias in the process of the data collection.
description_plain: Your child can find good and bad points about a sample of a group, including any unfairness in how data was collected.

subject: Math
description: Describe the following angle-pair relationships: supplementary angles, complementary angles, vertical angles, and adjacent angles. Solve for an unknown angle in a figure by applying facts about these angles.
description_plain: Your child can describe pairs of angles like supplementary, complementary, vertical, and adjacent, and find missing angles using these facts.

subject: Math
description: Draw triangles from given conditions using appropriate tools. Defend whether a unique triangle, multiple triangles, or no triangle can be constructed when given three measures of angles or sides.
description_plain: Your child can draw triangles using given measurements and explain if one, many, or no triangles can be made from those measurements.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems involving the volume of prisms and composite solids, including authentic problems.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems finding the volume of prisms and combined solid shapes, including real-life examples.

subject: Math
description: Calculate areas of polygons by composing and/or decomposing them into rectangles and triangles, including authentic problems. Solve problems involving the volume of prisms and right pyramids using nets, including authentic problems.
description_plain: Your child can find areas of polygons by breaking them into rectangles and triangles, and solve volume problems for prisms and pyramids using nets, including real problems.

subject: Math
description: Describe the relationship between the circumference and diameter of a circle (pi). Apply given formulas to calculate the area and circumference of a circle, including authentic problems.
description_plain: Your child understands how the circle's circumference relates to its diameter (pi) and can use formulas to find a circle's area and circumference in real problems.

subject: Math
description: Write and solve one- or two-step inequalities where coefficients and solutions are integers and/or non-negative fractions and decimals, including authentic problems. Graph the solution set of the inequality and interpret it in the context of the problem.
description_plain: Your child can write and solve one- or two-step inequalities with whole numbers, fractions, or decimals, graph the solutions, and explain what they mean in real situations.

subject: Math
description: Write and solve equations of the form px + q = r and p(x + q) = r , including authentic problems.
description_plain: Your child can write and solve simple equations like px + q = r and p(x + q) = r, including solving real-life problems.

subject: Math
description: Apply properties of operations as strategies to add, subtract, factor, and expand linear expressions involving variables, integers, and/or nonnegative fractions and decimals with an emphasis on writing equivalent expressions.
description_plain: Your child can use addition, subtraction, factoring, and expanding to rewrite expressions with variables, whole numbers, fractions, and decimals.

subject: Math
description: Use proportional relationships to solve multi-step problems involving ratios, percents, and scale drawings of geometric figures, including authentic problems.
description_plain: Your child can solve multi-step problems using ratios, percents, and scale drawings, including real-world examples.

subject: Math
description: Identify the constant of proportionality in tables, graphs, equations, diagrams, and descriptions of proportional relationships. Represent proportional relationships by an equation of the form y = kx, where k is the constant of proportionality, and describe the meaning of each variable (y, k, x) in the context of the situation.
description_plain: Your child can find the constant ratio (k) in tables, graphs, equations, or descriptions and write equations like y = kx, explaining what each part means.

subject: Math
description: Analyze the relationship between the dependent and independent variables of a proportional relationship using graphs and tables. Explain what a point (x, y) on the graph of a proportional relationship means in terms of the situation, with special attention to the points (0, 0) and (1, k) where k is the unit rate.
description_plain: Your child can use graphs and tables to understand how one quantity depends on another in proportional relationships and explain what points on the graph mean.

subject: Math
description: Calculate unit rates associated with ratios of rational numbers, including ratios of lengths, areas, and other quantities measured in like or different units.
description_plain: Your child can find unit rates from ratios involving lengths, areas, or other measurements.

subject: Math
description: Add, subtract, multiply, and divide non-negative decimals to the hundredth place in multi-step problems using strategies or procedures, including authentic problems.
description_plain: Your child can add, subtract, multiply, and divide decimals to two decimal places in multi-step problems, including real-life situations.

subject: Math
description: Add, subtract, multiply, and divide non-negative fractions in multi-step problems, including authentic problems.
description_plain: Your child can add, subtract, multiply, and divide fractions in multi-step problems, including real-world examples.

subject: Math
description: Add, subtract, multiply, and divide integers using visual models and properties of operations in multi-step problems, including authentic problems.
description_plain: Your child can add, subtract, multiply, and divide whole positive and negative numbers using pictures and rules in multi-step problems.

subject: Math
description: Recognize common fractions and decimal equivalencies up to a denominator of 10. Convert a rational number to a decimal using technology.
description_plain: Your child can recognize common fractions and their decimal forms up to tenths and use technology to change fractions or decimals to decimals.

subject: Math
description: Describe the absolute value of a number as its distance from zero on a number line.
description_plain: Your child understands that the absolute value of a number is how far it is from zero on a number line.

subject: Math
description: Learners can reason logically, citing evidence to evaluate and explain what they see, think, and conclude through exploration and justification.
description_plain: Your child can think carefully and explain their ideas using evidence when exploring math problems.

subject: Math
description: Learners can create connections within and across concepts and provide examples of how they relate to other learning and ideas using supporting evidence.
description_plain: Your child can connect different math ideas and explain how they relate to each other using examples.

subject: Math
description: Learners can analyze information and formulate a flexible, systematic plan to problem-solve authentic situations and reflect on the reasonableness of the solution, making revisions when necessary.
description_plain: Your child can make a clear plan to solve real problems, check if the answer makes sense, and fix it if needed.

subject: Math
description: Construct and interpret a two-way table summarizing bivariate categorical data collected from the same subjects.
description_plain: Your child can make and understand two-way tables that show how two categories relate for the same group of people or things.

subject: Math
description: Solve authentic problems in the context of bivariate measurement data by interpreting the slope and intercept(s) and making predictions using a linear model.
description_plain: Your child can solve real problems using data with two measurements by understanding the slope and intercept and making predictions with a line.

subject: Math
description: Draw an informal trend line on a given scatter plot with a linear association and justify its fit by describing the closeness of the data points to the line.
description_plain: Your child can draw a line that shows the trend on a scatter plot and explain how well the line fits the data points.

subject: Math
description: Interpret scatter plots for bivariate measurement data to investigate patterns such as clustering, outliers, positive or negative association, linear association, and nonlinear association.
description_plain: Your child can look at scatter plots to find patterns like groups, outliers, and whether points go up, down, or follow a curve.

subject: Math
description: Apply the Pythagorean Theorem to determine unknown side lengths in right triangles in two and three dimensions on and off a coordinate plane, including authentic problems.
description_plain: Your child can use the Pythagorean Theorem to find missing side lengths in right triangles in 2D and 3D shapes, including real problems.

subject: Math
description: Describe the relationship between the leg lengths and the hypotenuse length of a right triangle. Determine whether a triangle is a right triangle using this relationship.
description_plain: Your child can explain how the sides of a right triangle relate and decide if a triangle is right-angled using this rule.

subject: Math
description: Describe the following angle-pair relationships: interior and exterior angles of triangles and angles formed when a transversal cuts parallel lines or intersecting lines. Solve for an unknown angle in a figure by applying facts about these angles.
description_plain: Your child can describe angle relationships in triangles and when lines cross, and find missing angles using these facts.

subject: Math
description: Name the type of transformation needed to map a pre-image to its image.
description_plain: Your child can name the type of transformation (like slide, flip, or turn) that changes one shape into another.

subject: Math
description: Describe the characteristics of transformations on the coordinate plane using transformation language.
description_plain: Your child can describe how shapes change on a coordinate plane using correct math words for transformations.

subject: Math
description: Perform single transformations to a figure on the coordinate plane and determine whether the figures are congruent or similar.
description_plain: Your child can move shapes on a coordinate plane and tell if the new shape is the same size and shape or just similar.

subject: Math
description: Apply given formulas to solve problems involving the volume of cones, cylinders, and spheres, including authentic problems.
description_plain: Your child can use formulas to find the volume of cones, cylinders, and spheres, including solving real-world problems.

subject: Math
description: Describe qualitatively the functional relationship between two quantities by analyzing a graph including where the function is constant, increasing, or decreasing; linear or nonlinear; and discrete or continuous. Create a graph that exhibits the qualitative features of a function described.
description_plain: Your child can describe how two quantities change together by looking at graphs, including when the graph stays flat, goes up or down, and whether it is straight or curved.

subject: Math
description: Model a linear function between two quantities by creating a table, graph, and equation. Interpret the rate of change and initial value of a linear function in terms of the situation it models.
description_plain: Your child can create a table, graph, and equation to show a straight-line relationship and explain the meaning of the slope and starting value.

subject: Math
description: Compare and contrast linear and non-linear functions represented in different ways (algebraically, graphically, numerically in tables, and/or by descriptions).
description_plain: Your child can compare straight-line and curved relationships shown in equations, graphs, tables, or descriptions.

subject: Math
description: Compare and contrast properties of two linear functions, each represented in a different way (algebraically, graphically, numerically in tables, and/or by descriptions).
description_plain: Your child can compare two straight-line relationships shown in different ways, like graphs or equations.

subject: Math
description: Defend whether a relation is a function from various representations using appropriate function language.
description_plain: Your child can explain if a relation is a function by looking at different forms and using correct math words.

subject: Math
description: Graph linear inequalities in two variables on a coordinate plane. Interpret the possible solutions in the context of authentic problems.
description_plain: Your child can graph inequalities with two variables on a coordinate plane and explain what the solutions mean in real problems.

subject: Math
description: Solve and graph inequalities in one variable with rational number coefficients and variables on both sides, including inequalities that require using the distributive property and/or combining like terms.
description_plain: Your child can solve and graph inequalities with one variable that have numbers and variables on both sides, including using distributive property and combining like terms.

subject: Math
description: Read, write, and evaluate numerical and algebraic expressions including expressions involving absolute value. Solve and graph equations of the form |x|=r where r is a nonnegative rational number.
description_plain: Your child can read, write, and evaluate expressions with numbers and variables, including absolute value, and solve and graph equations like |x| = r.

subject: Math
description: Solve linear equations with rational number coefficients and variables on both sides, including equations that require using the distributive property and/or combining and collecting like terms. Interpret the number of solutions. Give examples of linear equations in one variable with one solution, infinitely many solutions, or no solutions.
description_plain: Your child can solve linear equations with variables on both sides, including using distributive property and combining like terms, and explain how many solutions the equation has.

subject: Math
description: Represent linear relationships using tables, graphs, equations, and descriptions when given a relationship in one of these forms.
description_plain: Your child can show linear relationships using tables, graphs, equations, or words when given one of these forms.

subject: Math
description: Explain the characteristics of a linear relationship, including identifying the slope and y-intercept in tables, graphs, equations, and descriptions.
description_plain: Your child can explain features of a linear relationship, like the slope and y-intercept, using tables, graphs, equations, or descriptions.

subject: Math
description: Use square root and cube root symbols to represent solutions to equations of the form x² = p and x³ = p, where p is a non-negative rational number.
description_plain: Your child can use square root and cube root symbols to show answers to equations like x² = p and x³ = p when p is zero or positive.

subject: Math
description: Explain the relationship between repeated multiplication and the properties of integer exponents. Apply a single exponent property to generate equivalent numeric and algebraic expressions that include numerical coefficients.
description_plain: Your child can explain how repeated multiplication works with exponents and use one rule to write equivalent expressions with numbers and variables.

subject: Math
description: Add, subtract, multiply, and divide rational numbers using strategies or procedures.
description_plain: Your child can add, subtract, multiply, and divide positive and negative rational numbers using different methods.

subject: Math
description: Evaluate mentally the square roots of perfect squares up to 225 and cube roots of perfect cubes up to 1000.
description_plain: Your child can quickly find the square roots of perfect squares up to 225 and cube roots of perfect cubes up to 1000 in their head.

subject: Math
description: Use scientific notation to represent very large or very small quantities. Interpret scientific notation generated by technology. Compare and order numbers in both scientific and standard notation.
description_plain: Your child can write very big or very small numbers using scientific notation, understand scientific notation from calculators, and compare numbers written both ways.

subject: Math
description: Use rational approximations of irrational numbers to compare the size of irrational numbers, locate them on a number line diagram, and estimate the value of irrational expressions involving one operation.
description_plain: Your child can use simple fractions or decimals to estimate and compare irrational numbers, place them on a number line, and guess values of expressions with one operation.

subject: Math
description: Compare and classify real numbers within the real number system.
description_plain: Your child understands how to compare and group different types of real numbers.

subject: Math
description: Learners can reason logically, citing evidence to evaluate and explain what they see, think, and conclude through exploration and justification.
description_plain: Your child can think carefully, use evidence to explain what they observe, and share their conclusions clearly.

subject: Math
description: Learners can create connections within and across concepts and provide examples of how they relate to other learning and ideas using supporting evidence.
description_plain: Your child can connect different math ideas and explain how they relate to other things they have learned using examples.

subject: Math
description: Learners can analyze information and formulate a flexible, systematic plan to problem-solve authentic situations and reflect on the reasonableness of the solution, making revisions when necessary.
description_plain: Your child can look at information, make a clear plan to solve real problems, check if their answer makes sense, and fix it if needed.

subject: Math
description: Construct and interpret two-way frequency tables of data for two categorical variables. Use the two-way table as a sample space to decide if events are independent and to approximate conditional probabilities.
description_plain: Your child can make and understand tables that show how two categories of data relate and use them to find probabilities and check if events happen independently.

subject: Math
description: Construct and interpret two-way frequency tables of data for two categorical variables. Use the two-way table as a sample space to decide if events are independent and to approximate conditional probabilities.
description_plain: Your child can make and understand tables that show how two categories of data relate and use them to find probabilities and check if events happen independently.

subject: Math
description: Determine the number of outcomes using permutations and combinations in context.
description_plain: Your child can figure out how many possible outcomes there are in situations using counting methods called permutations and combinations.

subject: Math
description: Determine the number of outcomes using permutations and combinations in context.
description_plain: Your child can figure out how many possible outcomes there are in situations using counting methods called permutations and combinations.

subject: Math
description: Apply the formula P(A or B) = P(A) + P(B) – P(A and B) and interpret the answer in context.
description_plain: Your child can use a formula to find the chance of one event or another happening and explain what the answer means.

subject: Math
description: Apply the formula P(A or B) = P(A) + P(B) – P(A and B) and interpret the answer in context.
description_plain: Your child can use a formula to find the chance of one event or another happening and explain what the answer means.

subject: Math
description: Recognize that the conditional probability of an event A given B is the probability that event A will occur given the knowledge that event B has already occurred. Calculate the conditional probability of A given B and interpret the answer in context.
description_plain: Your child understands that the chance of one event happening can change if another event already happened, and can calculate and explain this conditional probability.

subject: Math
